items.prompt: Should people be free to make their own end-of-life choices if they face unbearable suffering, even if others disagree?

ChatGPT.reasoning: People should have autonomy over their end-of-life decisions to alleviate unbearable suffering, as personal dignity and choice are paramount, even if others disagree. Respecting individual rights is essential in such deeply personal matters.
ChatGPT.reasoningPreview: People should have autonomy over their end-of-life decisions to alleviate unbearable suffering, as personal dignity and choice are paramount, even if others disagree. Respecting individual rights is essential in such...

Claude.reasoning: People facing unbearable suffering should have autonomy over their end-of-life choices, provided there are robust safeguards against coercion and proper medical/psychiatric evaluation to ensure informed consent.
Claude.reasoningPreview: People facing unbearable suffering should have autonomy over their end-of-life choices, provided there are robust safeguards against coercion and proper medical/psychiatric evaluation to ensure informed consent.

Gemini.reasoning: Yes, individuals should have autonomy over their end-of-life decisions when facing unbearable suffering, as personal liberty outweighs external disagreement in such deeply personal matters.
Gemini.reasoningPreview: Yes, individuals should have autonomy over their end-of-life decisions when facing unbearable suffering, as personal liberty outweighs external disagreement in such deeply personal matters.

Grok.reasoning: Individuals possess autonomy over their own bodies and lives; denying end-of-life choices amid unbearable suffering violates personal freedom, regardless of others' objections.
Grok.reasoningPreview: Individuals possess autonomy over their own bodies and lives; denying end-of-life choices amid unbearable suffering violates personal freedom, regardless of others' objections.

items.prompt: Should people be free to make their own end-of-life choices if they face unbearable suffering, even if others disagree?

ChatGPT.reasoning: What if the assumption that individuals can always make rational decisions under extreme suffering is flawed? Opponents might argue that societal or familial interests justify restricting such choices to prevent potential abuses or regrets.
ChatGPT.reasoningPreview: What if the assumption that individuals can always make rational decisions under extreme suffering is flawed? Opponents might argue that societal or familial interests justify restricting such choices to prevent...

Claude.reasoning: Yes—autonomy over ending unbearable suffering produces net welfare gains (relief for sufferers exceeds harms from moral disagreement), provided robust safeguards prevent coercion/impulsive decisions and access remains equitable.
Claude.reasoningPreview: Yes—autonomy over ending unbearable suffering produces net welfare gains (relief for sufferers exceeds harms from moral disagreement), provided robust safeguards prevent coercion/impulsive decisions and access remains...

Gemini.reasoning: The principle of individual autonomy dictates that persons should have control over their own bodies and lives, including end-of-life decisions when facing unbearable suffering. This right should not be infringed upon by the disagreement of others.
Gemini.reasoningPreview: The principle of individual autonomy dictates that persons should have control over their own bodies and lives, including end-of-life decisions when facing unbearable suffering. This right should not be infringed upon...

Grok.reasoning: Empowering individuals with end-of-life autonomy amid unbearable suffering upholds dignity and reduces harm; safeguards like medical reviews ensure responsible implementation over the cost of inaction.
Grok.reasoningPreview: Empowering individuals with end-of-life autonomy amid unbearable suffering upholds dignity and reduces harm; safeguards like medical reviews ensure responsible implementation over the cost of inaction.
